draft.introduction: The Vatican Sayings are a collection of Epicurean maxims discovered in a Vatican manuscript in 1888. They supplement the Principal Doctrines with practical wisdom about friendship, self-sufficiency, and the philosophical life.

translation.text: We are born once and cannot be born twice, but we must be no more for all eternity. You who do not control tomorrow postpone your joy; life is wasted in procrastination and each one of us dies without truly having lived. Of all the things that wisdom provides to make life entirely happy, much the greatest is the possession of friendship.
translation.original_text: γεγόναμεν ἅπαξ, δὶς δὲ οὐκ ἔστι γενέσθαι

translator_notes.note: The emphasis on friendship as essential to happiness distinguishes Epicureanism from the more self-sufficient ideals of Stoicism and Cynicism. For Epicurus, the philosophical community of friends was not incidental to the good life but its most important component.
